insight: For Belgium, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. In half of their breaks, they managed to gain momentum, with an average change of -0.050 minutes per minute, indicating a slight loss of pace but not a significant one. When Belgium was pressing before a break, they struggled to maintain their momentum, losing pace at an average rate of -0.200 minutes per minute, and in the one instance this happened, they failed to regain their footing. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually gained momentum at an average rate of +0.100 minutes per minute, suggesting that a more relaxed approach before a hydration break can help them come back stronger.
